cityDNA.coreFantasy: A vampiric undead city where Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater, and Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil turn cliffside, terraced slope, civic terraces, dry ground and granite, black marble, iron, pale marble stone, blackened iron into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Vel Mouraine should feel beautiful, nocturnal, and dangerous: a civic court where secrecy, ancestry, intelligence, containment, and theater overlap.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Vel Mouraine organizes public life around memory preserved with ceremony: its districts convert Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Vel Mouraine should be recognized by Granite, Black Marble, Iron, Pale Marble Stone, Blackened Iron, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Necromantic Preservation, Threshold Oaths, Road Warding, and the named anchors Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil, Black Market Arcade Beacon, and Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to vampiric undead; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, and the five section landmark families symbolize memory preserved with ceremony while warning against refinement hiding coercion.

canonLocks: Vel Mouraine is a vampiric undead city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil.
The Beacon is Black Market Arcade Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Underworld / Hidden Economy) preserves these exact anchor names: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
Section 2 (Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter) preserves these exact anchor names: Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Vault, Ancestor Masks Monument, Bone Lantern Path Nook.
Section 3 (Security / Intelligence) preserves these exact anchor names: Security / Intelligence Hall, Cipher Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Dossier Vault Nook.
Section 4 (Prison / Containment) preserves these exact anchor names: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.
Section 5 (Entertainment / Theater) preserves these exact anchor names: Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Vel Mouraine as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Vel Mouraine's environment should make Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

forms: iron and pale marble stone materials
skyline: Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Black Market Arcade Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater.

civilization.people: Vel Mouraine's people are vampiric undead; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Black Market Arcade Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Vel Mouraine immediately identifiable through Granite, Black Marble, Iron, Pale Marble Stone, Blackened Iron, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Necromantic Preservation, Threshold Oaths, Road Warding, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Black Market Arcade Beacon, Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Mist Veils, Lantern Lit, Civic Blue Glow, lanterns, symbolic civic object, hooded lanterns, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with cliffside, terraced slope, civic terraces, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

prestigious: workshops or offices near Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court
specialist houses serving Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of memory preserved with ceremony made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Granite, Black Marble, Iron, Pale Marble Stone, Blackened Iron, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground, and the vampiric undead visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater.
storyHooks: access to Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Black Market Arcade Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Vel Mouraine.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Underworld / Hidden Economy with anchors: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.
S2 must preserve Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter with anchors: Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Vault, Ancestor Masks Monument, Bone Lantern Path Nook.
S3 must preserve Security / Intelligence with anchors: Security / Intelligence Hall, Cipher Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Dossier Vault Nook.
S4 must preserve Prison / Containment with anchors: Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, Judgment Yard Nook.
S5 must preserve Entertainment / Theater with anchors: Entertainment / Theater Civic Hall, Opera Court, Entertainment Theater Lantern Monument, Mask Pavilion Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil as the skyline symbol, Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court at the central civic authority, and Black Market Arcade Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so Vel Mouraine does not converge with other cities: Underworld / Hidden Economy, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Security / Intelligence, Prison / Containment, Entertainment / Theater.

Use terrain cues (Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Dry Ground), material cues (Granite, Black Marble, Iron, Pale Marble Stone, Blackened Iron), and magic cues (Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Necromantic Preservation, Threshold Oaths, Road Warding) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Vampiric Roseglass Court of the Red Veil
Bone White Ceremony Veiled High Court
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
